title: XDG_DATA_HOME ignored except when creating initial configuration files
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman.1  documents XDG_DATA_HOME as

> Images are pulled under XDG_DATA_HOME when specified, otherwise in the home directory of the user under .local/share/containers/storage.

The phrase _Images are pulled_ seems to indicate that XDG_DATA_HOME is somehow connected with `podman pull`, but its value is used when podman initializes `storage.conf` to set the values of `storage.graphroot` and `storage.rootless_storage_path`.  That implies that it is more like podman's `--root` option.

In any case, once a `storage.conf` file exists,   it seems that `XDG_DATA_HOME` is ignored.

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
```
pkill podman

rm -rf $HOME/.local/share/containers $HOME/.config/containers/

export XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman
rm -rf $XDG_DATA_HOME

podman info --format='graphroot={{.store.GraphRoot}}{{"\n"}}configfile={{.store.ConfigFile}}'

podman pull alpine
podman images

export XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman2
rm -rf $XDG_DATA_HOME

podman info --format='graphroot={{.store.GraphRoot}}{{"\n"}}configfile={{.store.ConfigFile}}'
podman images

```
**Describe the results you received:**

```
+ pkill podman
+ rm -rf /home/dj/.local/share/containers /home/dj/.config/containers/
+ export XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman
+ XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman
+ rm -rf /tmp/podman
+ podman info '--format=graphroot={{.store.GraphRoot}}{{"\n"}}configfile={{.store.ConfigFile}}'
graphroot=/tmp/podman/containers/storage
configfile=/home/dj/.config/containers/storage.conf
+ podman pull alpine

Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob c9b1b535fdd9 done  
Copying config e7d92cdc71 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
e7d92cdc71feacf90708cb59182d0df1b911f8ae022d29e8e95d75ca6a99776a
+ podman images
 
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED       SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   e7d92cdc71fe   8 weeks ago   5.86 MB
+ export XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman2
+ XDG_DATA_HOME=/tmp/podman2
+ rm -rf /tmp/podman2
+ podman info '--format=graphroot={{.store.GraphRoot}}{{"\n"}}configfile={{.store.ConfigFile}}'
graphroot=/tmp/podman/containers/storage
configfile=/home/dj/.config/containers/storage.conf
+ podman images
 
REPOSITORY                 TAG      IMAGE ID       CREATED       SIZE
docker.io/library/alpine   latest   e7d92cdc71fe   8 weeks ago   5.86 MB

```
**Describe the results you expected:**

I expcted that changing `XDG_DATA_HOME` would change the runtime behavior.

i expected that podman would use the new value as the  directory to manage images.  Instead `podman info` shows the old directory is used, and `podman images` shows the images pulled into the old directory.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 1.8.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
